tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mild hypoglycemia, low HbA1c, and elevated creatinine, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), with low specific gravity. These findings indicate significant renal dysfunction, possible tubular damage, and metabolic derangements. Further nephrological and metabolic evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
